channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: ADA targets Cockpit Design Commonalities Across TEDBF, LCA AF Mk2, and AMCA for Enhanced Pilot Efficiency and Programme Synergy
item.description: India’s combat aircraft development programmes are achieving significant design convergence through shared cockpit architectures for the Twin Engine Deck-Based Fighter (TEDBF), LCA AF Mk2 (also known as Tejas Mk2 or Medium Weight Fighter), and the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA). This strategic approach reduces development time, lowers costs, and ensures a standardised, highly intuitive pilot interface across naval, air force, and next-generation stealth platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF’s 60 MTA Transport Aircraft Plan Linked to Secure Weapon Exports as Delivery Role Expands
item.description: India’s planned induction of a new 60-ton class Medium Transport Aircraft (MTA) as a replacement for the ageing An-32 fleet is increasingly being shaped not just by operational logistics requirements, but also by the country’s expanding defence export ambitions. Sources indicate that the Ministry of Defence (MoD) has asked the Indian Air Force (IAF) to factor in an additional strategic role for the platform—secure aerial delivery of high-value weapon systems to partner nations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA to Get Stealth-Optimized NATO-Standard Refuelling Probe: ADA Tender Signals Global Interoperability Push
item.description: The Aeronautical Development Agency’s (ADA) latest tender for NATO-standard refuelling probe adaptors marks a critical milestone in the development of the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA), underscoring India’s focus on stealth, interoperability, and future-ready aerial refuelling capability. Issued under Tender ID 139393335, with a submission deadline of May 1, 2026, the requirement forms part of a broader high-priority program to design and qualify an Actuated Retractable Refuelling Probe for India’s fifth-generation fighter.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAI Unveils Next-Gen Touchscreen MFD for HAL Rudra, Promises Fighter-Like Cockpit Efficiency and Reduced Pilot Workload
item.description: Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI) has showcased a next-generation touchscreen multi-function display (MFD) concept tailored for HAL’s Rudra armed helicopter, introducing a modern cockpit architecture designed to streamline operations, reduce crew workload, and enhance mission effectiveness. The system reflects a shift toward smartphone-like human-machine interfaces in military aviation, where intuitive interaction and information consolidation are becoming central to cockpit design.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Indian Startup SatLeo Labs Develops Space-Based Thermal Satellites to Detect Chinese and Pakistani Missile Launches in Real Time
item.description: An Indian startup is advancing into the strategic domain of space-based missile detection with the development of thermal observation satellites designed to identify launches in real time across conflict zones and global hotspots. Ahmedabad-based SatLeo Labs, founded in 2023, is building a constellation of 15 satellites equipped with advanced infrared sensing payloads aimed at both defence and civilian applications.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: EON Space Labs Unveils Germanium-Free Lumira_E40I50 ISR Payload, Tackles India’s Critical Thermal Imaging Dependency
item.description: Hyderabad-based deep-tech startup EON Space Labs has launched the Lumira_E40I50, an indigenously developed infrared imaging payload for drones that stands out for eliminating one of the most critical materials in thermal optics—germanium. The breakthrough comes at a time when India’s defence ecosystem is grappling with supply chain vulnerabilities tied to imported critical materials, particularly germanium, whose prices have surged dramatically since mid-2023 due to export controls and geopolitical tensions.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: SSS Defence “Naga” Suppressors Gain Momentum After Indian Army Induction, Set for Global Expansion in 2026
item.description: Engineered to reduce acoustic signature while enhancing weapon control, the “Naga” range of suppressors developed by SSS Defence represents a significant advancement in India’s indigenous small arms ecosystem. Designed for compactness without compromising performance, the system focuses on reducing muzzle blast, improving recoil management, and delivering consistent shot placement—key parameters for modern combat and tactical operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India tests Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile (LR-AShM), Remains Mum as no official announcement comes.
item.description: The Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile (LR-AShM), developed for the Indian Navy, is a hypersonic glide weapon capable of engaging both static and moving targets. It uses indigenous avionics and high-accuracy sensors, following a quasi-ballistic trajectory with speeds reaching up to Mach 10 and maintaining an average of around Mach 5 through multiple skips. Its low-altitude flight, speed and manoeuvrability make it difficult to detect by enemy radar systems.
item.dc:creator: USI

item.title: Green safeguards woven into economic, defence objectives of Great Nicobar project: Centre
item.description: Sharing details of the strategic project, the Union government said it seeks to transform Great Nicobar into a strategic maritime and economic hub by leveraging its proximity (about 40 nautical miles) to the East–West shipping route and reducing dependence on foreign transshipment ports keeping in view the defence and national security purpose.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: India and Armenia discuss current geostrategic situation, expanding military engagement
item.description: “Lieutenant General Edvard Asryan, Chief of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Republic of Armenia, called on General Upendra Dwivedi, COAS. The interaction focused on the current geostrategic situation, strengthening India-Armenia Defence Cooperation, expanding military engagement and advancing collaboration in areas of mutual interest,” Indian Army’s Additional Directorate General of Public Information (ADGPI) stated on X.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: As Terror Networks Converge Across West Asia, India Emerges As A Trusted Balancer
item.description: In an interaction titled “Crisis in the Middle East: Are We Entering a New Regional Order?”, Egyptian parliamentarian Mohamed M Farid told think-tank Usanas Foundation CEO, Dr Abhinav Pandya, that India’s role as a constructive partner will be welcomed in the region.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Pakistan Eyes Big Kashmir Strike To Trigger Global Intervention
item.description: Pakistan is planning a massive attack and would hope that India would retaliate like the way it did during ‘Operation Sindoor’. Considering the new-found ties with the US under President Donald Trump, Pakistan would hope for an external intervention.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Great Nicobar Project key to maritime security, reducing foreign dependence: Major Gen (Retd.) Arvind Bhatia
item.description: Speaking to IANS on the project’s geopolitical relevance, Bhatia underlined that proximity to the Strait of Malacca — one of the world’s busiest maritime chokepoints — places India in a stronger position to monitor international trade and strategic movements, particularly those involving China.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Govt notifies changes to Citizenship Rules
item.description: In a gazette notification published on Thursday, the government has added a specific proviso for citizenship applicants involving children that ‘the minor child cannot at any time hold the passport of any other country while also holding the Indian passport’.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Terrorist hideout busted in Kishtwar; weapons, explosive recovered
item.description: Officials said it belonged to a terrorist commander whom the troops had eliminated in the past.
item.dc:creator: PTI
